justifications.clarity: Prose flows naturally and transitions smoothly between all five main sections; the GDPR Article 22 'A. Petrov' example concretely grounds an abstract legal obligation in a realistic, reader-identifiable compliance scenario, and context boxes effectively frame each section's purpose before technical detail begins.
justifications.rigor: Four formal definitions, four numbered equations (FPR/PPV, RRF fusion, AMI, XBRL retrieval score), and 15+ citations provide solid formal grounding; the claim that global AML compliance cost exceeded $270 billion 'before the advent of widespread AI adoption' is unsourced, and the 95-99% FPR statistic rests on a single FinCEN citation where additional independent empirical studies would strengthen the argument.
justifications.completeness: All five outline sections—regulatory landscape, AML, KYC/sanctions, regulatory reporting, and governance—are fully covered; the beneficial ownership subsection enriches the chapter beyond the original plan; the significant gap is the complete absence of Python code examples or notebook references, which is a notable omission for a practitioner-facing book explicitly targeting engineers and data scientists.
justifications.pedagogy: Seven clearly stated learning objectives open the chapter and are systematically addressed; difficulty progresses logically from regulatory frameworks through technical architecture to governance; three worked examples (GDPR Article 22, agentic screening, MRM governance) support understanding; the chapter lacks any exercises (beginner/intermediate/advanced) and code-based learning activities that the target audience of engineers and quants would benefit from.
justifications.style: Writing is authoritative and non-formulaic throughout; the custom context, definition, remark, and example tcolorbox environments are used correctly and consistently; notation is uniform across all four equations; no paragraph-heading misuse; occasional long multi-clause sentences (e.g., the 80-word sentence in Section 1 enumerating five overlapping regulatory frameworks) are the only minor stylistic friction.
